revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}}|上円下方墳}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/miyazukakofun.html Kumagaya City official site] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumagaya]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Joenkahofun]]
#redirect[[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[Category:dd]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}}|上円下方墳}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high

It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}}|上円下方墳}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}}|上円下方墳}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}}|上円下方墳}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}}|上円下方墳}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}}|上円下方墳}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/miyazukakofun.html Kumagaya City official site] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/miyazukakofun.html Kumagaya City official site] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kumagaya]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Joenkahofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:imported Kofun]]Miyazuka Kofun[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[Category:11 3]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Miyazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/miyazukakofun.html Kumagaya City official site] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kumagaya]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Joenkahofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:imported Kofun]]Miyazuka Kofun[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Miyazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Miyazuka Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=draft}} | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|06|N|139|20|43|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=draft}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[burial chamber]] or presence of any [[grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/miyazukakofun.html Kumagaya City official site] {{in lang|ja}}
Miyazuka Kofun

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Miyazuka Kofun]][[ja:宮塚古墳 (熊谷市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454036]] Wikidata name: Miyazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.15161111|N|139.34519444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[:en:Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] or presence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Miyazuka Kofun]][[ja:宮塚古墳 (熊谷市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454036]] Wikidata name: Miyazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.15161111|N|139.34519444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[:en:Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] or presence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Miyazuka Kofun]][[ja:宮塚古墳 (熊谷市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454036]] Wikidata name: Miyazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.15161111|N|139.34519444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya, Saitama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[:en:Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] or presence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/miyazukakofun.html Kumagaya City official site] {{in lang|ja}}

It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] or presence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high

The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] in the middle reaches of the [[:en:Arakawa River (Kantō)|Arakawa River]], and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] or presence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/miyazukakofun.html Kumagaya City official site] {{in lang|ja}}

It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] or presence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|en|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|WD=Q5770745|lt=Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station}} on the {{ill|Chichibu Railway|en|Chichibu Railway|WD=Q843780|lt=Chichibu Railway}} {{ill|Chichibu Main Line|en|Chichibu Main Line|WD=Q5096033|lt=Chichibu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kumagaya, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Miyazuka kofun | native_name =宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumagaya Miyaduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyazuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.15161111|N|139.34519444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya, Saitama]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace}} in the middle reaches of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa River}}, and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] or presence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|en|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|WD=Q5770745|lt=Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station}} on the {{ill|Chichibu Railway|en|Chichibu Railway|WD=Q843780|lt=Chichibu Railway}} {{ill|Chichibu Main Line|en|Chichibu Main Line|WD=Q5096033|lt=Chichibu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/miyazukakofun.html Kumagaya City official site] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Miyazuka Kofun'''|宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hirose neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Kumagaya, Saitama|Kumagaya]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209810|title=宮塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyazuka Kofun is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} in the middle reaches of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|qid=Q625465|lt=Arakawa River|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}}, and is part of the Hirose tumulus cluster, which has many densely packed small-scale tumuli. It is a rare example of a {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} tumulus, which has a square base on top of which is a domed tumulus. It was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or towards the final stages of the Kofun period. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] or presence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is unknown. Although the base was originally square, it has been partially cut away by local farmers.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|en|Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|qid=Q5770745|lt=Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station|1=Hirose-Yachō-no-Mori Station}} on the {{ill|Chichibu Railway|en|Chichibu Railway|qid=Q843780|lt=Chichibu Railway|1=Chichibu Railway}} {{ill|Chichibu Main Line|en|Chichibu Main Line|qid=Q5096033|lt=Chichibu Main Line|1=Chichibu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Upper domed portion: 10 meter diameter x 2.5 meter high ;Lower rectangular base: 17 meters wide on east x 24 meters wide on west x 2 meters high
